raw_consumer_statement:
During XXXX XXXX XXXX I was traveling in XXXX XXXX XXXX so I had booked a XXXX XXXX  flight with XXXX XXXX  XXXX XXXX to XXXX via XXXX XXXX for XX/XX/XXXX. Then the XXXX XXXX  started and XXXX finally cancelled the flight but rescheduled me unto a similar flight again for XX/XX/XXXX. However, the night before the flight on XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX emailed me to tell me that they 'Could Not Accommodate ' me on the rescheduled flight. This was quickly followed by another email from XXXX offering me a voucher for the original flight ticket. 
According to the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  Airlines are obliged to provide a CASH Refund for cancelled flights. 
When I tried to book another flight with XXXX from XXXX XXXX to the XXXX which was similar to the original flight that I had booked, I discovered that XXXX had increased the price from the original {$790.00} to a minimum of {$2400.00}. I responded by email informing XXXX that the voucher was unacceptable and that I wanted a refund to my Credit Card. They did not respond. 
In the mean time all flights back to the XXXX had increased significantly. I end up flying to the XXXX from XXXX XXXX and paid over {$1300.00} for a flight from XXXX to XXXX departing XXXX weeks later which was almost twice the normal price, however this was my cheapest option. The whole inconvenience cost me at least {$3000.00}. 
It was impossible to contact XXXX by phone whilst in XXXX XXXX so I phoned a XXXX number for XXXX when I returned to the XXXX. I ended up speaking to someone in the XXXX. I asked for a refund which they refused hence this is why I filed a dispute via my Credit Card with Citibank, however their conclusion was that a voucher from XXXX was acceptable so no cash refund.
